observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a.) For the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned personnel (by issuing
an executive order) in ensuring the full implementation of the one-time
cleansing of PPE;
b.) For the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Physical Inventory
Plan and ensure completion of the physical inventory-taking of all
properties;
For the Municipal Accountant:
c.) Prepare, update, and maintain subsidiary records like the PPE ledger cards
for each item of property and equipment of the municipality;
For the General Services Officer (GSO):
d.) To maintain property cards for each PPE item in order to facilitate
reconciliation and control;
e.) Replace the damaged/illegible property tag with a sticker/tag that could
withstand exposure to the elements; and
f.) Properly monitor the renewal of motor vehicle registration to avoid future
penalties.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the management:
a) Reclassify the amount of ₱35,152,903.20 back from Accounts Payable to its
proper account under Current Portion of Loans Payable – Domestic and ensure
that both the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial
Statements for the subsequent years reflect the correct classification and
disclosure; and
b) Disclose all relevant information regarding financial liabilities in the Notes to
Financial Statements including, but not limited to, the aging schedule of
payables, details of significant loan agreements, and breakdown of current and
non-current portions. Please refer to Annex A for specific reference and
guidance.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
To Municipal Accountant:
a) Efficiently prepare adjusting journal entry/ies to recognize all the valid
reconciling items.
To Municipal Treasurer:
b) Daily monitor all cash books to ensure accuracy and timeliness; and
c) Capacitate staff with the pertinent guidelines on the proper handling, custody
and judicious preparation of the said cashbook.
To Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
d) Perform regular reconciliation of the accounts to avoid the accumulation of
unreconciled items in the books; and
e) Conduct a meeting with the LBP Midsayap branch manager for confirmation of
the unreconciled bank errors.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
a) For the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile
their records on allotments and obligations regularly so that any resulting
discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected; and
b) For the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA
and/or in the Notes to FS the:(i) changes between the original and final budgets;
(ii) material differences between the budget and actual amounts; and (iii)
reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to
enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
a) Strengthen the alignment of planning documents by ensuring that both the
Annual Budget and the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are prepared and
approved within the prescribed period of the Synchronized Planning and
Budgeting Calendar for LGUs;
b) Sustain and institutionalize the effective utilization of the 20% development
fund by continuing to prioritize implementation-ready projects, programs, and
activities (PPAs). This ensures that the fund contributes meaningfully to socio-
economic and environmental outcomes, in accordance with the intent of DBM-
DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020; and
c) Improve the timeliness of project implementation by addressing factors
causing delays and strengthening project execution strategies. This will help
ensure that PPAs funded under both current and continuing appropriations are
completed within their scheduled timelines for more effective delivery of public
services.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
a) Optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF to lessen the socio-economic and
environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks,
including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and
response capabilities of the community;
b) Set a specific and achievable implementation target period for each PPA of the
LDRRMF in the LDRRMP and AIP; and
c) Implement a detailed budget specification and needs assessment process with
enhanced monitoring mechanisms.

analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the

of said accounts;
c. Adopt a credit risk policy and monitor the exposure to credit risk on an on-going
basis; and determine the impairment in accordance with IPSAS and adjust its
respective allowance for impairment account; and
d. Undertake a comprehensive review of the existing collection strategies to improve
the collection of receivables, reduce impairment losses, and enhance the
accuracy of your financial reporting.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Implement regular monitoring to track transfers to the STF and conduct review of
transfers to the Special Trust Fund to identify any irregularities;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to transfer the
unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund and Mitigation Fund-Maintenance
and Other Operating Expenses from the general fund to the trust fund as
prescribed. Henceforth, transfer the yearly unutilized balance of DRRM Fund at
the end of the year to reflect the correct balance of the trust liability account at
every accounting period; and
c. Ensure submission of the required documents related to the utilization of various
projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Reclassify the current portion of Loans Payable–Domestic for better presentation
of liabilities of the LGU in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-
end/reporting date; and

the subsequent years for appropriate understanding.

a. Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its
PPE;

physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in planning, execution, and reconciliation of GSO and
Accounting records;

and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation and conduct further investigation on the variances noted during the
actual count;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure of relevant information in
the Notes to Financial Statements for the subsequent years for appropriate
understanding;
e. Prepare the appropriate journal entries and other accounting records to rectify the
errors and present the asset accounts to their proper carrying amount;

Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the
accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular
No. 2015-008 and submit the pertinent reports as prescribed by existing laws,
rules, and regulations;
g. Prepare the appropriate journal entries and other accounting records to correct
the account classification and secure supporting documents to substantiate the
set-up of payables to ensure accurate financial records and proper accounting
procedures; and
h. Give priority to the directives set under the COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts which will provide benefits to the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges and
formulate a more effective, efficient, sustainable, and responsive revenue and
receivable collection procedures and strategies; and
b. Tap additional sources of income as authorized under RA 7160 to increase locally
sourced revenues and lessen its dependence on the internal revenue allotments.

We recommended and the Management agreed to do the following:

examination of documentary requirements to verify the integrity and

the regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of
the receivables and determine the concerned debtors and accountable officers for
strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts;
b) Adopt a credit risk policy and monitor the exposure to credit risk on an on-going
basis; and
c) Determine the impairment in accordance with IPSAS and adjust its respective
allowance for impairment account.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following courses of actions:
a) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges;
b) Tap additional sources of income as authorized under RA 7160 to increase
revenues and avoid total dependence on the internal revenue allotments; and
c) Strictly implement the tax ordinances enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan relative
to revenue generation program.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed to
the following courses of actions:
a) Ensure that the 20% development fund be optimally utilized for socio-economic
and environmental outcomes pursuant to the intent of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 by prioritizing the
projects, programs and activities which are attainable/implementation-ready to
ensure efficient and effective fund governance;
b) Reinforce and/or adjust existing planning and budgeting practices and techniques
to further improve the orderliness of the Municipality’s processes to be able to
comply with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs; and
c) Formulate realistic and attainable work plan by installing performance tracking
mechanisms to monitor the implementation of existing projects and taking into
consideration the factors affecting performance identified in the monitoring
process.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed of
the following courses of actions:
a) Ensure orderliness in the planning and budget processes, as demonstrated by
compliance with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs;
b) Prepare the MDRRMFIP in accordance with the form specified in Annex A of COA
Circular No. 2012-002;
c) Ensure the preparation and submission of a more complete and comprehensive
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan which shall
include under a separate caption and detailing the lists of Projects, Programs and
Activities (PPAs) that will serve as the basis for the disbursement of DDRMF.
d) Assign specific timelines for the identified DRRM PPAs to evaluate achievement
rate and identify areas with major shortfall or needs improvement in order to make
timely and periodic remedial measures;
e) Management should take concrete actions to address the issues on delayed or
non-implementation of funded PPAs, taking into consideration the new normal
brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, thereby ensuring resiliency despite the
current challenges; and
f) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entry for the
recording of the unexpended portion of DRRMF, and carefully prepare and review
financial reports to enhance its reliability and accuracy.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to the following courses of actions:
a) Promote media literacy to assist constituents in identifying fake news in relation
with COVID-19 vaccines and develop a communication strategy that includes clear
and concise information about the safety and efficacy thereof;
b) Collaborate with Barangay Officials and those who have influence in areas with a
high number of unvaccinated individuals, such as community leaders, tribal
leaders, or religious leaders to obtain their support in promoting COVID-19
vaccination and to address challenges and concerns within specific communities
thereby increasing vaccine acceptance and uptake; and
c) Implement strategies to address any factors contributing to vaccine wastage such
as enhancing Information Education Campaign for COVID-19 vaccination and offer
incentives to encourage participation in the vaccination program.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed
to the following courses of actions:
a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of
the said reports to the COA audit team;
b) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were
provided; and
c) Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation and establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to come up
with a more GAD responsive and related programs and activities.

In view of the foregoing observation, we recommended and the management agreed
to undertake more capability building on gender and development and ensure that the
members of the GAD Focal Point System attend trainings on gender mainstreaming,
gender analysis, formulation of gender-responsive programs and conduct of gender
audit, and institutionalizing GAD database or sex-disaggregated data in the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the General Services Officer to maintain updated Property Cards
per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of
Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records, and thereafter as the
transaction occurs;
b. Require both offices to reconcile property records ensuring that the balances
shown in the prepared RPCPPE agreed with the supply records and the
book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa;
c. For any resulting difference, the Accounting and the General Services Office
should work out on tracing the reconciling items, establish its
existence/nonexistence and determine from there any further action that may
be undertaken;
d. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every
three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared
RPCPPE.
e. Ensure that no PPE is distributed to its end-user without the property tags
with complete information such as, tag inventory number, property number,
serial number of the property, description and other salient information.
f. Prepare and submit reconciled copy of the RPCPPE to the COA Auditors on
or before the 30th day of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the management immediately undertake the disposal
procedures of unserviceable properties and equipment as mentioned in Section 79 of P.D.
1445 and Section 448 of the GAAM, Volume II. Submit copy of the request for disposal
together with the IIRUP duly prepared for revaluation/assessment by the Audit Team.

We recommended that management send a written notice to the payee of the
existence of the check and make necessary adjustments based on the results of
notice/verification so as to present the correct balances of affected accounts. Inform the
Municipal Treasurer of reconciling items noted in the BRS that require adjustment in the
cash book. Correspondingly subsidiary ledgers should be maintained for ready
reference.

We recommended the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and prioritize the
construction of sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall
be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said
act.

We recommended that the management:
a. Intensify and implement measures to collect the past and demandable
receivable. Send demand letters and give specific deadline to compel the
proponents to settle their accounts with the LGU;
b. Instruct concerned LGU officials/personnel on the periodic monitoring of funds
transferred and utilized;
c. Institute legal action, if necessary, against defaulting POs; and
d. See to it that the requisites for entitlement to government funds by NGOs/POs
shall be strictly complied with and ensure that the awarding of project is covered
by a MOA of which the terms of reference under COA Circular No. 2007-001
are incorporated.

We recommended that the Management maximize the services of ten (10) present
revenue collection officers and stop the practice of assigning the tasks of collecting to
personnel hired as job orders.

We recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions
on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from the
personnel's payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted
loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the
collecting agents/office of these creditors.
Ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least P5,000.00 for CY
2018 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act of 2018.

We recommended that management require the Municipal’s Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the
head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and
Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days
after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General Services
Officer/Supply Officer to furnish COA copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders and
inspection reports within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and
inspection.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirements set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act
No. 10410 by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for
various activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and
comply with the guidelines on allowable charges against the Special Education
Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1. S.2017 of DepEd, DBM, and
DILG; and
b. Conductconsultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers, and other stakeholders of the ECCD
System in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD
Programs.

We recommended that the management explore all the possibilities to resolve the
issues and problems encountered for the operation of MIOT for possible utilization of the
MIOT as planned to avoid more wastage of government resources in the
construction and maintenance of the building and structures. Further, the local chief
executive should ensure that extensive and accurate planning, evaluation and analysis
are undertaken on priority programs/projects/activities so that public funds are spent
economically, efficiently and effectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Management to endeavor the timely implementation of the BuB projects.  LGU should be motivated by the incentives afforded by the government to ensure the on time implementation and delivery of services to the local constituents.
Also, we recommend the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) to persistently review and validate the status of their BuB projects as presented in the OpenBub portal.  LPRAT should regularly do so to ascertain the condition of the pending or the “not yet implemented” status projects and be able to address properly the cause of the suspension or non implementation.

We recommend the Management to ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20% development fund shall partake the nature of capital expenditure and investment and record corresponding asset account/s in the financial statements.

We recommend that Management ensure that the PPAs under the Mitigation Fund and those under the Special Trust Fund are provided with details in the LDRRMFIP.  Likewise the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared and submitted to the COA Auditor.

We recommend the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

We recommend the management to:
Consider the blacklisting or suspension of suppliers who submit procurement requirements containing false information or falsified documents.
Require the authorized canvasser to do a legitimate canvass as prescribe in the procurement law.
Present to the Team for inspection the two units printer #810.
Issue and maintain ARE every time there is a purchase or transfer of equipment from one accountable officer to another to prevent future problems in pinpointing accountability and responsibility on the issued equipment and to avoid abuse or misuse of the same.
Strictly observe the rules and regulations as embodied in COA Circular No. 2012-003, RA 9184 and its Revised IRR, and other existing rules and regulations on government procurement to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government and to prevent possible disallowances for such violation.

We recommend that management send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check and make necessary adjustments based on the results of notice/verification so as to present the correct balances of affected accounts.  Inform the Municipal Treasurer of reconciling items noted in the BRS that require adjustment in the cash book.

We recommend the LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV right away for the corresponding adjustment entry of the correct amount due from taxpayers for basic and additional levy on real property taxes as well as the fines involved. Also, instruct the Accountant further that in the event that e-NGAS updates is not still in place, an adjusting entry at the end of the every year be immediately taken up to bring the amount supplied by the system to a correct figure.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency.  Moreover, we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan enjoin to impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or by judicial auction to enforce the collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P186,841,628.27.

We recommend that the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following:
The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking.  A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and
The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.
We recommend that the Management ensure all real estate of the LGU is registered under the Torrens Title System and responsible for their proper and effective use and management.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position as of a given period of time.

We recommend the management to update the system/program used in accounting for withholding taxes concerning the increase of tax exclusions from P30,000 to P82,000 for the “13th month Pay and other benefits” in accordance with the BIR Rules and Regulations.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to properly account and report the Gross Compensation Income, Non-Taxable/Exempt Compensation Income, and Taxable Compensation Income and subsequently compute the correct Tax Dues for the same.
Submit a copy to the Office of the Auditor as evidence of payment of the additional taxes remitted.

We recommend that the management explore all the possibilities to resolve the issues and problems encountered for the operation of MIOT for possible utilization of the MIOT as planned to avoid more wastage of government resources in the construction and maintenance of the building and structures.  Further, the local chief executive should ensure that extensive and accurate planning, evaluation and analysis were undertaken on priority programs/projects/activities so that public funds are spent economically, efficiently and effectively.

We recommend that the Management designate a fish inspector and establish a fish terminal in accordance with the economic enterprise ordinance enacted primarily for purposes of generating income and protecting the public.

We recommend the Management to establish one (1) public slaughterhouse in an area allowed under the zoning ordinance that will serve the need for a fast, efficient and sanitary slaughtering facility in the municipality, the neighboring municipalities/cities and some parts of the country as well.  Also, we recommend the Management to take every opportunity to improve economic condition of the municipality for the benefit of the inhabitants.

We recommend that Management revisit its Ordinance No. 235, Series of 2012 and other related local laws for the proper implementation of the Economic Enterprise Code of the Municipality of Midsayap.  Also, we recommend that Management enforce closure of existing slaughterhouses which failed to secure franchise and license to operate within the prescribed period.  Moreover, Management has to take every opportunity to improve economic condition such collecting authorized fees and charges for the benefit of the inhabitants.

We recommend that Management revisit its Ordinance No. 235, Series of 2012 for the proper implementation of the Economic Enterprise Code of the Municipality of Midsayap.  Also, we recommend that Management enforce sanction/s for any violation of terms and conditions and other applicable laws, ordinances, rules and regulations such as cause for the cancellation or revocation of the Contract of Lease, and the simultaneous closure and /or padlocking of the stall.

We recommend that Management direct the Accountant to draw JEV right away to take up the accrued revenues from rental fee of stalls and commercial building as receivables from delinquent renters based on the established record of the treasury office.  The entry of receivables in the books of accounts would substantiate the validity and existence of the delinquency.  Also, we recommend that the Municipal Treasury Office, Economic Enterprise Office and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive collection strategies to collect the long overdue arrearages of P36.1M.  Moreover, we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan enjoin to impose the penal provisions, if necessary for proper implementation of the related local laws.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:
Adhere strictly to the guidelines prescribed in the grant of CNA Incentives duly supported with documents necessary in the payment of the same.
Direct the LGU employees who received such incentives to refund payment thereof, if such claims were not properly justified hence, proven to be invalid.
We recommend that Management adhere strictly to the guidelines provided on the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the LGU to adhere to the existing national standards/guidelines, laws and policies such as the timeliness of the project implementation stipulated in DILG and OPAPP Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2, Series of 2014, as well as  mindful and strict in the execution of the provisions of the contract or agreement entered into with other parties.

Also, we recommend the LGU to monitor and oversee closely the physical implementation of the projects to ensure that it is in compliance with the plans such as specifications and the timeliness to complete.

We recommend that management liquidate promptly the previously funds transferred by DSWD FO as stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement in order that the next cycle of the feeding program is accomplished as scheduled.  The focal person should be mindful of her duties and responsibilities for the timely implementation of the program. Likewise, proper planning, close monitoring and coordination of the implementing offices should be done so that the programmed project will be implemented on time.

We recommend that Management instruct the focal person for LDRRMC to formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the local development councils (LDCs); to prepare annually and submit to the local sanggunian through the LDRRMC and the LDC the proposed programming of the LDRRMF e.g. LDRRMFIP; and to furnish the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the DILG-LGOO a copy each of the annual LDRMFIP and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommend that the municipal government integrate its GAD programs, projects and activities that are in its DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget in its Annual Investment Program, Local Expenditure Program, Budget Expenditure and Sources of Financing, and in the Executive Budget pursuant to Item 4.1.C.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the municipal government submit its GPB for the ensuing year to the PPDO and then from the PPDO to the DILG Regional Office for review and pursuant to Item 4.1.C.6.2 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the municipal government, during the preparation of its GAD Accomplishment Report, administer the HGDG test on its GAD PPAs for CY 2015 as basis in determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD budget pursuant to Item 4.1.C.4.5.e of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the municipal government create its GAD M&E Team or expand the functions and membership of the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to include the GFPS TWG Chair, Human Resource Management Officer, Budget Officer, representative/s of the LCE, civil society organizations representative/s specifically from women’s organizations and the academe pursuant to Item 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to:

Prepare Contract of Service/Job Order stipulating the specific duties and responsibilities to be performed by the job order worker and other necessary provisions and conditions set forth in the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM)-Volume I, Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and other Personnel Actions, Rule XI, Local Government Code Republic Act 7160, and other pertinent rules and regulations as this should become the basis in the assessment and evaluation of performance for the re-hiring of the personnel.

Require the Job Orders/Contractual personnel to prepare and submit Accomplishment Reports, together with the duly accomplished Daily Time Record (DTR), in support to the Payroll claims for the services rendered for the period. 

Submit the Certification required in the COA Circular 2012-001 stating that the activities/services cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the agency thus necessitating the need of hiring job order workers. 

Verify, together with the Accounting and other responsible offices, completeness and accuracy of documents prior to processing the transactions and submission thereof to COA Office. In relation to this, ensure receipts of payments are properly acknowledged by respective payees and documents forwarded to the office are original copies. 

Devise policies and guidelines in the hiring of job order workers in conformance to the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and other Personnel Actions, Rule XI, Local Government Code Republic Act 7160, COA rules and regulations as it this would form as a vital basis for the hiring of personnel under Job Order arrangements.

We recommend the Management to determine priority projects/programs/activities to be included in the annual performance budget and to implement within the calendar year or as scheduled to avoid reversion and or transfer of funds from one project/program to another.  The Management shall at all times adhere to the policy guideline set forth for fiscal administration with paramount consideration of delivery of basic services and benefits to the public.

We recommend the Management to strictly adhere with the guidelines embodied in COA Circular No. 2013-04.  The Management has to ensure that all resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguard against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, with a view to ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of government.

To require the Municipal Accountant to stop the processing of claims without complete documentation.

We recommend that the Local Executive Officer require the General Services Officer or any authorized official to regularly submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels with accomplished Drivers’ Trip Ticket for each vehicle/equipment used, including the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor for verification, monitoring and auditing of fuel, oil and lubricants used in accordance with COA Circular 77-61.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive should instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to prepare the payrolls while the Municipal Accountant should supervise the preparation of payrolls and ensure compliance to the aforementioned provisions of GAA. 

We further recommend that management discontinue the practice of deducting payments of monthly loan amortizations of private lending establishments from the personnel’s salary and wages payroll. Those personnel whom have loan agreement with private lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/s or office of respective lending establishments.

We recommend that the Local School Board, in collaboration with the LGU:
1) Prepare a comprehensive financial plan applying a strategic planning process; and

2) Adhere strictly to the established goals and objectives of the Special Education Fund.
